methodsAttempted.query: site:warwick.ac.uk Warwick generative AI policy guidance students assessment
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official student academic-integrity guidance, assessment-design guidance, a login-gated institutional PDF, faculty-level pages, and research guidance.
Firecrawl search found the public student artificial-intelligence academic-integrity page and the AI information compliance policy.
Firecrawl search found official AI in research, responsible-use, and information-compliance pages with data-protection content.

artifacts.summary: Discovered official Warwick sources for student AI academic-integrity guidance, assessment design, research guidance, responsible and ethical use, and AI information compliance. The institutional approach PDF and academic-integrity framework pages redirected to Warwick Web Sign-On and were not accessed. Faculty or department pages were recorded but excluded from university-wide claim evidence.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official student academic-essentials page on warwick.ac.uk with explicit AI academic-integrity guidance for students.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:warwick.ac.uk Warwick generative AI policy guidance students assessment
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Academic Development guidance page with assessment-design guidance for generative AI.
Official Research & Impact Services guidance on AI in research, approved by the Research Governance and Ethics Committee according to the page.
Official Academic Development page stating Warwick's responsible and ethical AI use approach.
Official Information Management Policy Framework page for Warwick AI information compliance.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:warwick.ac.uk Warwick generative AI policy guidance students assessment
artifacts.verificationNotes: Public official page, but its directly visible policy content is specific to Academic Development Centre courses and points to a login-gated institutional PDF.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Institutional Approach to the Use of Artificial Intelligence and Academic Integrity
artifacts.queryUsed: site:warwick.ac.uk Warwick generative AI policy guidance students assessment
artifacts.verificationNotes: Firecrawl and HTTP fetch redirected to Warwick Web Sign-On. Login-gated PDF was not accessed and was not used as evidence.
Official Warwick Law School guidance. Excluded from university-wide claim candidates because it is faculty-specific.
Official History Department PGT guidance. Excluded from university-wide claim candidates because it is department-specific.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The purpose of this policy is to establish ground rules for the use of generative AI tools in completing assessments and critical incident questionnaires on credit-bearing and/or Advance HE accredited courses provided by the Academic Development Centre.
artifacts.notes: The page is official and relevant, but its visible policy scope is the Academic Development Centre. The linked institutional PDF was login-gated.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: You must sign in to view this page.
artifacts.notes: The PDF was not accessed because the official URL redirected to Warwick Web Sign-On.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: Warwick Law School is committed to developing a holistic approach to AI in all aspects of our education.
artifacts.notes: Faculty-specific guidance was not used for university-wide claims.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: This page explains the History Department's approach to Artificial Intelligence (AI).
artifacts.notes: Department-specific guidance was not used for university-wide claims.

artifacts.errorReason: Redirected to Warwick Web Sign-On login page; source not accessed.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's student-facing guidance says students may use AI only within requirements set out in assessment briefs and course handbooks, which may restrict or prohibit AI use.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Student AI use depends on assessment and course requirements

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The general position is that you can use Artificial Intelligence (AI) but must follow any requirements set out in assessments and course handbooks. Those requirements may restrict or prohibit the use of AI.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Student AI use is conditional on assessment and course requirements.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Key principles for using AI / Check the rules
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow student-guidance claim directly supported by the quoted official Warwick page.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's student-facing guidance says students are required to state whether AI was used in the submission process and explain why, where, and how it was used.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Students must disclose AI use when used in submissions

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You will be required to state if any AI has been used as part of the submission procedure. If you use an AI, you must set out why, where and how you have done so.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students must state AI use and explain why, where, and how it was used.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Key principles for using AI / Declare it
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim is limited to the student-facing disclosure rule quoted from the official Warwick page.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's student-facing guidance tells students not to enter personal or confidential data into AI tools unless they understand what will happen to the data, and recommends Copilot chat with a Warwick account for that kind of data.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Student AI privacy guidance directs caution and recommends Warwick-authenticated Copilot chat

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not input any personal or confidential data into any AI tools, unless you fully understand what will happen to that data. The university recommends using Co-Pilot chat for this kind of data - when logged in with your Warwick account.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students are told not to enter personal or confidential data into AI without understanding data handling.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Key principles for using AI / Protect privacy
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a privacy-specific student guidance claim directly supported by the quoted source text.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's assessment-design guidance says generative AI use in student submissions needs thoughtful support so responsible use and clear demonstration of human achievement are maintained.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Assessment design guidance calls for supported responsible AI use

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: use of generative AI technologies to produce text and other media as part of student submissions ... needs to be thoughtfully supported to ensure responsible use and clear demonstration of human achievements.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Assessment guidance says AI use in submissions needs support for responsible use and clear human achievement.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Designing assessment for achievement and demonstration of learning outcomes
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a teaching and assessment-design guidance claim. It does not state a binding student rule.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's AI in research guidance says its principles apply to all researchers and researchers must consider AI-related research risks including integrity, information security, and accountability risks.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI research guidance applies to all researchers and risk categories

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The principles set out in this guidance apply to all researchers as previously defined. Researchers must consider all risks that may be relevant to their research that arise from AI.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Warwick AI research guidance applies to all researchers and requires consideration of AI-related research risks.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Who is expected to use this guidance / Risk Considerations
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow research-guidance claim supported by the source's scope and risk-consideration text.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's AI in research guidance says researchers are responsible for misconduct-type practices involving AI, including improper handling of information or use of another person's ideas, even if such practices occur inadvertently through an AI tool.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Researchers remain responsible for AI-enabled research misconduct risks

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Misconduct in research includes: the fabrication or falsification of research data; improper handling of information on individuals collected during research; or the use of another person's ideas, work or research data without appropriate acknowledgement. Researchers are responsible for any such practices, even if they occur inadvertently through the use of an AI tool.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The research guidance keeps researchers responsible for AI-enabled misconduct-type practices.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Breach of Responsibilities and Expected Standards
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim follows the source's research misconduct examples and AI responsibility sentence without extending beyond research context.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's responsible-use guidance frames responsible AI use as honest, ethical, transparent, human-accountable, safe, secure, and attentive to bias, fairness, inclusivity, and accessibility.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Responsible AI use principles emphasize human accountability, transparency, safety, and fairness

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Responsible use includes ... using AI with honesty, in ethical and defensible ways, with human responsibility taken for the outcomes; being transparent about AI use; making concerted efforts to maximise safe and secure use; using AI in ways that mitigate bias and promote fairness, inclusivity and accessibility.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Responsible AI use is framed around honesty, human responsibility, transparency, safety, security, fairness, inclusivity, and accessibility.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Responsible use includes the following aspects
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a high-level responsible-use guidance claim; wording avoids making it a disciplinary rule.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's AI Information Compliance Policy covers everyone with a contractual or implied relationship with the University and all information processed by the University.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI information compliance policy applies to members and university-processed information

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The policy covers everyone who has a contractual (formal or informal/implied) relationship with the University, including employees, students, visiting academics, and consultants. The policy covers all information processed by the University, regardless of ownership or format.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The AI information compliance policy applies to covered University members and University-processed information.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim is a direct scope statement from the official AI information compliance policy.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's AI Information Compliance Policy says all new uses of AI tools, products, or services must go through appropriate procurement processes, regardless of cost.
artifacts.normalizedValue: New AI tool uses require appropriate procurement processes

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Regardless of costs, all new uses of AI tools and services/products or services, must go through appropriate procurement processes.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: New AI tool or service uses must go through appropriate procurement processes regardless of cost.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the procurement requirement is directly stated in the official policy.

artifacts.claimText: Warwick's AI Information Compliance Policy says certain data, including personal or confidential material, University intellectual property, and some copyrighted or third-party data, must not be put into AI software without prior approval.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Restricted data must not be entered into AI software without prior approval

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Certain types of data must never be put into any AI software, without prior approval from IDG or the Research Ethics Committee. These include: Passwords and usernames. Personally identifiable information (PII) or other sensitive or confidential material. Any data related to University Intellectual Property.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Restricted data types must not be put into AI software without prior approval.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a privacy/data-restriction claim directly grounded in the official policy. The claim lists representative categories rather than all categories.

artifacts.title: The University of Warwick AI Policy Staged Report - 2026-05-12
artifacts.summary: The staged crawl found public official Warwick guidance covering student academic-integrity expectations for AI, assessment-design guidance, AI in research guidance, responsible-use principles, and the IMP 02 AI Information Compliance Policy. Ten claim candidates were machine-reviewed as directly supported by public official source text. The institutional approach PDF and academic-integrity framework page redirected to Warwick Web Sign-On and were not used as evidence; faculty and department AI pages were recorded but excluded from university-wide claim candidates.
limitations: This report is based on public official sources retrieved for run uapt-the-university-of-warwick-20260512.
The institutional approach PDF and academic-integrity framework page redirected to Warwick Web Sign-On and were not accessed.
Faculty and department AI guidance pages were not used for university-wide claim candidates.
This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
